what_it_does: A JSON design-token registry that declares a single source-of-truth visual system: font imports, a root CSS extra, one primitive color, and grouped semantic tokens (colors, typography, shape, effects) intended to be compiled into ../design-system.css.
observations: The file opens with a self-description saying it is the source of truth and that emit.py compiles it into ../design-system.css.
Tokens use two shapes: {"v": value} for literal values and {"ref": <primitive>} for indirection through the primitives map.
The only primitive is "gold" (#e6ab5c), which is referenced by acc, ok, kind-process, and gold-mid.
Google Fonts imports for Fraunces, Bricolage Grotesque, IBM Plex Mono, and IBM Plex Sans are listed verbatim.
root_extra sets a single CSS property: font-size: 14px.
Groups cover surface tiers, signal/semantic colors, ink on bright fills, node-type colors, typography, living-instrument effects, and shape/elevation.
Extensive inline notes record design decisions, dates, author initials (Tim), and rationale such as the two-vivid + one-muted color split.
Persona pastel tokens for Atlas and Nova are defined but explicitly noted as not currently live.
Typography tokens are named by role (fs-micro, fs-tag, fs-meta, fs-body, fs-lg, fs-title, fs-display) and font role (font-display, font-mono, font-body, font-sans).
Shape tokens were rationalized onto a 4-grid space/radius scale and include shadow-color, shadow, elev-1, and elev-2.
standouts: Primitive/reference architecture centered on a single gold value that cascades through accent, ok, process, and mid-gold tokens.
Inline prose fields (_what, _palette_note, per-group note) turn the file into a decision ledger, not just a value table.
Persona pastel system (Vi/Atlas/Nova) defined as future-switch hooks while the app currently runs only the default.
Semantic, role-based type and shape scales intended to replace scattered literal values across the codebase.
conventions: Follows a declarative token-registry pattern with grouped categories and primitive references.
Follows extend-by-registration: adding a token means adding an entry; adding a category means adding a group.
Follows self-documenting data by embedding long explanatory notes inside JSON values.
Uses CSS-ready value strings (gradients, rgba, font stacks, box-shadows) directly as token values.
Breaks strict separation of metadata and data by mixing design essays with token definitions.
Uses semantic naming (acc, await, fail, kind-process) rather than literal color names for most tokens.
concerns: Large inline prose in JSON values complicates automated parsing and validation.
Persona tokens for Atlas and Nova are defined but not live, creating potential dead weight until a switch mechanism exists.
Inconsistent use of primitive references: kind-present repeats the gold value literally instead of referencing the gold primitive.
root_extra is a single ad-hoc CSS property not integrated into the token model.
Hardcoded external Google Fonts URL may break offline or air-gapped builds.
No visible schema or version field for the registry format itself.
notes: This file is both a machine-compilable design-token registry and a human-readable decision log for a warm, gold-primary instrument UI.
questions: Where is emit.py and how does it resolve refs, groups, root_extra, and imports into CSS?
Which consumers reference each token, and are all defined tokens actually used?
How are persona tokens intended to be swapped in the running app (e.g., via a data-persona attribute)?
Is there a schema or validator that enforces the {"v"} vs {"ref"} token shapes?
Why does kind-present use a literal gold value rather than {"ref": "gold"}?
What is the relationship between this file and any other CSS or token files in the project?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose: it is a source-of-truth token registry compiled to CSS. It also carries extensive embedded design rationale and future persona definitions that go beyond a pure value table.
apparent_themes: Modular, role-based scales for type and space
intention_signals: To centralize the visual language so one primitive change propagates everywhere, to encode the warm dark instrument design direction in code, and to prepare a future persona-switching path without hardcoding it now.
novelty: The file embeds lengthy dated design rationale directly inside JSON metadata fields, and it defines persona pastel sub-families within a single gold-primary world while keeping them explicitly non-live.
contribution: A primitive/reference indirection model centered on gold; a semantic token vocabulary for surfaces, signals, node kinds, typography roles, and shape; CSS-ready value strings and font imports; embedded design rationale; and future persona-switch hooks.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON design-token registry that defines a warm gold-primary visual system with primitive references, semantic color groups, role-based typography, modular shape tokens, and embedded design rationale for compilation into a CSS file.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single source of truth for the product's look by declaring tokens that compile into CSS and by recording the design decisions behind them.
